center.name: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦
center.summary: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括毛脚鵟、Willow flycatcher、Bell's vireo等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|right |Least Bell's vireo with leg band, grasped in human hand. Bell's vireo (Vireo bellii) is a songbird that migrates between a breeding range in Western North America and a winter range in Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Sclater]], 1862 | species = bewickii | authority = (Audubon, 1827) | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = 1–2 dozen living, 2 recently extinct; see article text | synonyms = *Thryomanes leucophrys *Thryothorus bewickii *Thryothorus brevicauda *Thryothorus brevicaudus *Thryothorus bairdi | range_map = Thryomanes bewickii map.svg | range_map_caption = }} thumb|right|Illustration from John James Audubon|Audubon's [[The Birds of America]] Bewick's wren (Thryomanes bewickii) is a wren native to North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Lincoln's sparrow (Melospiza lincolnii) is a small sparrow native to North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2021 |title=Myadestes townsendi |volume=2021 |article-number=e.T22708587A139385014 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2021-3.RLTS.T22708587A139385014.en |access-date=1 November 2025}} | genus = Myadestes | species = townsendi | authority = (Audubon, 1838) | range_map = Myadestes townsendi map.svg }} Townsend's solitaire (Myadestes townsendi) is a medium-sized thrush, the only solitaire native to America north of Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: traillii, Connecticut | genus = Empidonax | species = traillii | authority = (Audubon, 1828) | range_map = Empidonax traillii map.svg | range_map_caption = Approximate distribution map }} thumb |right|Summer breeding and winter ranges of willow flycatcher subspecies from United States Geological Survey|USGS southwestern willow flycatcher survey protocol The willow flycatcher (Empidonax traillii) is a small insect-eating, migrant bird of the tyrant flycatcher family native to North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in arctic and subarctic regions of North America, Europe, and Asia during the breeding season, and migrates south for the winter. Historically, it was also known as "rough-legged falcon" in such works as John James Audubon's The Birds of America
